name: consolidate-skills-directory
trigger_phrases: ProjectMnemosyne has both plugins/<category>/<name>/ and skills/<name>/ directories
New contributors are confused about where to put skills

name: constant-centralization
trigger_phrases: Two or more modules define the same constant with the same value
A utility module (e.g., layer_testers.mojo) defines constants that semantically belong in a dependency (e.g., gradient_checker.mojo)

name: container-image-security-patching
trigger_phrases: Trivy scan step fails with Total: N (HIGH: X, CRITICAL: Y)
docker-build-timing or ci-image workflow fails on all branches (not just your PR)

name: contributing-md-structure-sync
trigger_phrases: A GitHub issue reports that CONTRIBUTING.md project structure listing is out of sync with the codebase
After adding a new scylla/ sub-package, to update documentation

name: conv2d-backward-analytical-value-tests
trigger_phrases: Need to verify exact gradient values, not just shapes or finite-difference proximity
Adding batch>1 backward tests — grad_bias and grad_weights must accumulate over batch

name: conv2d-gradient-checking-finite-differences
trigger_phrases: conv2d_backward tests only verify shapes/bias but not grad_input or grad_weights numerically
Existing gradient-checking files are at their per-file test limit and a new dedicated file is needed

name: corpus-surgical-correction-batch
trigger_phrases: Post-review correction pass on 20+ documents
Multiple documents share the same root-cause error (e.g., wrong constant propagated from a shared context)

name: coverage-threshold-tuning
trigger_phrases: CI fails with "Coverage failure: total of X% is less than fail-under=Y%"
Setting up coverage enforcement for the first time

name: cpp-atomic-posix-socket-adl-collision
trigger_phrases: A class has std::atomic<int> or std::atomic<uint16_t> members (e.g., server_fd_, port_)
The class also calls POSIX socket functions: bind(), accept(), listen(), getsockname(), setsockopt(), close(), poll()

name: cpp-ci-sanitizer-tsan-data-race-fixes
trigger_phrases: TSan CI job reports WARNING: ThreadSanitizer: data race with failing tests
A test hangs under TSan/ASan causing the CI job to hit its wall-clock timeout (job killed, not failed)

name: cpp-clang-tidy-deps-header-filter-fix
trigger_phrases: clang-tidy CI flooded with warnings from third-party headers in _deps/ (FetchContent)
.clang-tidy has HeaderFilterRegex: '(include|src|test)/' and project uses FetchContent

name: cpp-coverage-container-pinned-clang-format
trigger_phrases: gcovr coverage threshold check failing because ENABLE_COVERAGE CMake option is declared but no --coverage flags are applied
C++ test target links only the version library but not the actual server sources (store, routes, nats_client)

name: cpp-httplib-lambda-capture-ub
trigger_phrases: Registering cpp-httplib route handlers via a register_routes(server, store, nats) helper function
Route handlers that capture shared state (Store, NatsClient, etc.)

name: cpp-nlohmann-json-value-nullptr-type-error
trigger_phrases: json.value("key", nullptr).is_null() causes compile error
Error: "member reference base type 'std::nullptr_t' is not a structure or union"

name: cpp-pr-stale-duplicate-commit-detection
trigger_phrases: A PR fails all sanitizer CI jobs identically (asan + tsan + ubsan + lsan), not just TSan
The failure test is the same across every sanitizer (logical error, not a data race)

name: cpp-scaffold-cmake-test-path-fix
headings_h2: ## Known Non-Issue: "No tests were found" After Fix
trigger_phrases: ctest reports "No tests were found!!!" even though the test executable was built
test/CMakeLists.txt has add_executable(..._tests test/src/test_main.cpp)

name: create-review-checklist
trigger_phrases: Starting PR review for unfamiliar change type
Need specific checklist for code vs tests vs docs

name: create-swe-bench-tests
trigger_phrases: Setting up AI agent evaluation - Need benchmark tasks based on real coding problems
Creating test suites from PR history - Want to use actual merged PRs as evaluation tasks

name: credential-mount-context-manager
trigger_phrases: Directories like ~/.app-temp-creds-<uuid> or ~/.app-temp-<uuid> accumulate in home after container runs
shutil.rmtree() in a finally block silently fails (bare except Exception: pass) for temp dirs used as Docker volumes

name: cross-repo-dep-pypi-publish-fix
trigger_phrases: Consumer repo PRs fail with lock-file not up-to-date with the workspace or ModuleNotFoundError
A shared utility library (hephaestus, keystone, etc.) has new code on main but isn't tagged/published

name: cross-repo-python-utility-port
trigger_phrases: A file found in a worktree (orphaned, abandoned, or uncommitted) implements something similar to what exists in a shared utilities repo
Two repos have diverged implementations of the same pattern (circuit breaker, retry, rate limiter)

name: cytoscape-edge-visibility-optimization
trigger_phrases: A Cytoscape graph with many edges renders slowly (pan/zoom/layout stutter)
Nodes or edges are accidentally draggable/selectable in a read-only visualization

name: cytoscape-filter-compose
trigger_phrases: You have multiple Cytoscape.js visibility filters that must compose (AND logic: show only elements passing ALL filters)
A team/path highlight function calls clearTrajectory() internally, wiping prior filter work

name: cytoscape-frontend-rewrite
trigger_phrases: A Cytoscape.js layout uses globalRow * ROW_HEIGHT (fixed row height) and produces huge vertical gaps between match nodes at different times
You need to pack match nodes by time-slot and court (dense grid) rather than sparse row-based layout

name: cytoscape-interaction-patterns
trigger_phrases: Node click in Cytoscape triggers activateTrajectory() (which hides elements) but you want click to be a visual-only highlight (dim others, keep everything visible)
cytoscape-node-html-label HTML overlays (.match-card, .ranking-card divs) remain visible on screen even after their Cytoscape node's display is set to 'none'

name: dataloader-reset-verification
trigger_phrases: You need to prove an iterator-consuming loop calls reset() before iteration
A validation/training loop accepts a pre-existing loader that may be partially consumed

name: debugging-blog-bug-validation-methodology
trigger_phrases: Reviewing blog posts or incident reports that document specific bugs
Verifying historical bug fixes are still in place after refactoring

name: debugging-list-oob-dynamicvector-migration
trigger_phrases: Mojo code crashes with "execution crashed" and the file uses List[T]() followed by index assignment
DynamicVector->List migration changed DynamicVector[Int](N) to List[Int]() but kept vec[i] = val

name: debugging-loggeradapter-process-dict-mutation
trigger_phrases: A LoggerAdapter subclass overrides process() and calls extra.update() on the caller's dict
Context from one log call leaks into subsequent calls because the same extra dict is reused

name: debugging-radiance-graph-load-capture-browser-api-artifacts
trigger_phrases: The Radiance UI hangs after Analyze or Open
A run is marked succeeded but no graph appears in Model Explorer

name: debugging-silent-error-suppression-pola
trigger_phrases: A function catches an exception and falls back to a default instead of propagating the error
Symmetric functions (e.g., load/save, encode/decode, serialize/deserialize) have inconsistent error handling

name: debugging-slice-view-bad-free-destructor
trigger_phrases: ASAN reports attempting free on address which was not malloc()-ed
The freed address is N bytes INSIDE a larger allocation (e.g., "512 bytes inside of 1024-byte region")

name: deduplicate-issues
trigger_phrases: Repository has accumulated duplicate issues from automated issue creation workflows that create duplicates
Issues are already resolved but not closed

name: deduplicate-llm-json-extraction
trigger_phrases: Identical or near-identical code in multiple modules (3+ occurrences)
Bug exists in one implementation but not others with same logic

name: deduplicate-test-fixtures
trigger_phrases: Test fixtures contain many copies of the same file across different test directories
You notice the same markdown/config content duplicated in 10+ locations

name: defensive-analysis-patterns
trigger_phrases: Building statistical analysis pipelines that process experiment data with varying quality
Source data cannot be modified - must make analysis code robust instead

name: deferred-doc-placeholder
trigger_phrases: A docs navigation index (docs/index.md or similar hub) had a section removed because the linked files were placeholder stubs deleted under YAGNI
A follow-up issue explicitly tracks restoring the navigation hub when real docs are written

name: delegates-to-file-existence-check
trigger_phrases: You have a directory of agent .md files with YAML frontmatter that includes delegates_to fields
You want CI to automatically block PRs that introduce stale agent references

name: delete-placeholder-docs
trigger_phrases: A GitHub issue asks to delete empty/placeholder documentation files
Files contain only placeholder text like "Content here." or similar boilerplate

name: dependency-consolidation-pixi-single-source
trigger_phrases: Python dependencies are declared in multiple files (pixi.toml, pyproject.toml, requirements.txt) with divergent version constraints
Pinned versions in requirements*.txt fall outside pixi.toml range constraints (e.g., pytest==8.4.2 vs <8)

name: deprecated-file-stub-cleanup
trigger_phrases: A file is annotated DEPRECATED, # legacy, or # compatibility shim
A cleanup GitHub issue requests deleting a specific file or directory

name: deprecation-warning-migration
trigger_phrases: Deprecating a plain @dataclass that needs a Pydantic BaseModel replacement
Adding a runtime DeprecationWarning to a legacy type while keeping backward compatibility

name: depthwise-conv2d-gradient-checking-tests
trigger_phrases: depthwise_conv2d_backward has shape tests but no finite-difference gradient checks
Adding tests for all three gradient fields: grad_input (perturb x), grad_weights (perturb kernel), grad_bias (analytical)

name: detect-code-smells
trigger_phrases: Code review before merging
Identifying refactoring priorities

name: diverged-branch-cherry-pick-fix
trigger_phrases: git push fails with "non-fast-forward" on a feature branch
git status shows "Your branch and 'origin/<branch>' have diverged"

name: doc-audit-policy-violations
trigger_phrases: A policy violation is discovered in documentation examples and you want to check whether similar violations exist elsewhere
A new policy rule is added to CLAUDE.md and you need to verify no existing docs contradict it

name: doc-config-drift-check
trigger_phrases: You have numeric thresholds or config paths documented in markdown files (CLAUDE.md, README.md) that must stay in sync with authoritative config (pyproject.toml)
A manual audit has already caught documentation staleness at least once and you want to automate detection

name: doc-contradiction-resolution
trigger_phrases: Two project docs give contradictory guidance on the same topic (e.g., labels, branch naming, commit format)
CLAUDE.md contains an explicit policy rule that conflicts with an example in CONTRIBUTING.md

name: doc-generate-adr
trigger_phrases: Making significant architectural decisions
Choosing between technical alternatives

name: doc-issue-readme
trigger_phrases: Starting work on a GitHub issue
Documenting implementation approach

name: doc-stale-future-improvements-audit
trigger_phrases: A "Future Improvements" or "Future Work" section in documentation describes something that's already implemented
Systematic doc auditing is needed after a period of active development

name: doc-sync-existing-impl
trigger_phrases: An issue says "investigate X / fix Y" but reading the codebase reveals Y was already fixed
CLAUDE.md or docs/dev/*.md describes tool invocation that no longer matches the actual hook entry

name: doc-update-blog
trigger_phrases: Completed major milestone
Finished implementation phase

name: doc-validate-markdown
trigger_phrases: Before committing documentation
Markdown linting errors in CI

name: docker-build-timing-ci-measurement
headings_h2: ## Docker Build Timing: Source-Only Change Cache Efficiency
trigger_phrases: A CI build has a claimed performance characteristic (e.g., "source-only changes are 30% faster") that is not verified by any automated measurement
You need to add a before/after timing comparison to a CI workflow

name: docker-buildx-multiarch-local-image
trigger_phrases: A GitHub Actions CI job builds a base image with buildx, then builds a vessel/child image that uses it
The vessel build fails with pull access denied for <base-image> or not found

name: docker-entrypoint-bats
trigger_phrases: A Docker entrypoint shell script needs BATS test coverage
The script validates environment variables with complex regex patterns

name: docker-mojo-uid-mismatch-crash-fix
trigger_phrases: mojo run or mojo test crashes with exit code 134 before printing any user output
Error message contains: terminate called after throwing an instance of 'std::filesystem::__cxx11::filesystem_error'

name: docker-multistage-build
trigger_phrases: Large Docker images with build tools in production (gcc, g++, build-essential, make)
Python projects requiring compiled C extensions (cryptography, pillow, numpy, pandas)

name: docker-optional-dep-layer-caching
trigger_phrases: A Dockerfile uses a python3 -c "import tomllib..." snippet in Layer 2 to cache [project].dependencies
The project has [project.optional-dependencies] groups (e.g. analysis, dev) in pyproject.toml

name: docker-pixi-isolation
trigger_phrases: CI test jobs fail with unable to locate module 'std' inside Docker containers
Host environment tools (pixi, Mojo) leak into Docker containers via bind mounts

name: docker-precommit-entrypoint-install
trigger_phrases: Your project workspace is bind-mounted into the container (e.g., -v $(pwd):/workspace) rather than COPYd during build
pixi run pre-commit install is in your Dockerfile but hooks are not running at commit time

name: docker-to-podman-migration
trigger_phrases: Migrating from Docker to Podman in a project's CI/CD infrastructure (GitHub Actions or local Makefile)
Eliminating NATIVE=1 workarounds that bypass container-based test/build execution

name: dockerfile-absent-pkg-helper
trigger_phrases: Two or more test methods contain the same re.search(r"apt-get install.*\bpkg\b", ...) pattern
Adding a new test for a removed apt/cargo/rustc dependency

name: dockerfile-cache-arg-ordering
trigger_phrases: A Dockerfile uses ARG to control which optional dependencies are installed (e.g., ARG EXTRAS="" consumed in a pip install RUN)
Static Dockerfile tests exist but do not yet assert build-arg cache ordering

name: dockerfile-cargo-free-regression
trigger_phrases: A cargo, rustc, or other heavy build-time dependency was removed from Dockerfiles in favor of a pre-built binary
You want to block future contributors from re-adding the dependency without noticing

name: dockerfile-cargo-to-prebuilt-binary
headings_h2: ## Decision Criteria: Where to install the binary
trigger_phrases: Dockerfile has cargo install <tool> and that tool provides an official binary installer
Docker builds are slow due to Rust compilation (cargo install compiles from source)

name: dockerfile-env-version-pin
trigger_phrases: When a Dockerfile uses curl -fsSL https://<tool>/install.sh | bash without a version pin
When Dockerfile.ci already pins the version but the main Dockerfile does not

name: dockerfile-extras-validation
trigger_phrases: When a Dockerfile Layer uses a python3 -c snippet to parse pyproject.toml and resolve optional-dependency groups from an EXTRAS build-arg
When a typo in EXTRAS (e.g. EXTRAS=analyysis) would silently produce only runtime deps with no warning

name: dockerfile-from-inline-comment
trigger_phrases: Docker build fails with FROM requires either one or three arguments
A Dockerfile has an inline comment after the image tag or SHA256 digest on the FROM line

name: dockerfile-layer-caching
trigger_phrases: Python package with pyproject.toml — project uses a PEP 517 build backend (hatchling, flit, setuptools, etc.)
Source changes more often than dependencies — typical active development cadence

name: dockerfile-tomllib-fallback
trigger_phrases: When a Dockerfile builder stage parses pyproject.toml using tomllib and you need it to work on Python 3.10 (e.g. musl/Alpine/minimal variants)
When following up a documentation-only approach (see dockerfile-python-version-guard) with an actual code fallback

name: dockerfile-version-guard
headings_h2: ## Decision Criteria: Which version to pin to
## Decision Criteria: Fallback Code vs. Documentation (Python version constraints)
trigger_phrases: A Dockerfile has pip install --no-cache-dir <package> without a version specifier
A Dockerfile uses a Python stdlib module with a minimum version requirement (e.g., tomllib >= 3.11) and you need a regression guard against base image downgrades

name: docs-status-fix
trigger_phrases: A GitHub issue asks to update a "Current Status" or project phase description in CLAUDE.md
The status in CLAUDE.md contradicts the README or actual codebase state (CI workflows, test counts, Docker setup, published results)

name: docstring-fragment-validator
trigger_phrases: A quality audit falsely flags wrapped docstring sentences as fragments
You want to automate docstring quality checking at commit time without false positives

name: docstring-only-change
trigger_phrases: A GitHub issue requests documenting existing implicit behaviour in a Python module docstring
The change is pure docstring prose — no function signatures, tests, or logic changed

name: docstring-update-workflow
trigger_phrases: A follow-up issue is opened after a function signature change (e.g., "update example after #NNNN")
The module-level docstring shows a call that omits a now-required argument (e.g., missing step_fn)

name: docstring-view-vs-copy-clarification
trigger_phrases: A docstring says "returns a view" or "shares memory" but the implementation allocates fresh memory and copies bytes
A flag like _is_view is set to True after calling self.copy() (or equivalent), making the flag technically correct but the prose confusing

name: document-copy-vs-view-semantics
trigger_phrases: A cleanup issue references NOTE: Current implementation creates copies, not views in tests or source
A test function name says test_slice_is_view but the test body asserts not _is_view (name contradicts assertion)

name: document-deferred-future-improvements
trigger_phrases: A design doc contains a Future Improvements (or similar) section with bare list items
Items have been confirmed unimplemented via code inspection

name: document-hook-glibc-incompatibility
trigger_phrases: A pre-commit hook fails (or auto-skips) on some host OSes due to system library version constraints, not code errors
A wrapper/compat script already handles the incompatibility gracefully but there is no discoverable explanation in CONTRIBUTING.md

name: document-magic-number-constants
trigger_phrases: A file has inline NOTE: value=X for <reason> (see #NNNN) comments that are hard to discover
The same magic number appears in 2+ locations with the same comment copy-pasted

name: document-normalization-test-gotcha
trigger_phrases: A normalization layer backward test produced a false gradient mismatch (analytical ~0, numerical ~0.009, apparent ~1000x mismatch) that is NOT a bug
Closing a GitHub issue requesting documentation of a known test pathology

name: documentation-architecture-research-quality-rubric
trigger_phrases: Auditing AI architecture research documents for structural and content quality
Grading research proposals against the standard 6-dimension rubric

name: documentation-citation-gap-web-search-workflow
trigger_phrases: A LaTeX paper contains assertions without \cite references nearby.
Review feedback flags unsupported claims that need academic backing.

name: documentation-corpus-myrmidon-parallel-remediation
trigger_phrases: Research corpus is unpublished and contains change-note / correction-history / backward-compatibility prose that must be deleted (not annotated or moved to changelogs)
Defects span 20+ files and multiple repair classes (arithmetic, terminology, structure, citations)

name: documentation-ecosystem-role-drift-detection
trigger_phrases: External architecture docs describe a project's role differently than its actual implementation
Need to decide between "update docs" vs "add code" when documentation and implementation diverge

name: documentation-latex-paper-audit-methodology
trigger_phrases: A LaTeX paper is being prepared for submission and needs a final strict audit
The paper references external data files (CSV, JSON) and you need to verify every hardcoded number matches its source

name: documentation-multi-file-ecosystem-sync
trigger_phrases: Ecosystem documentation lists wrong number of repos or incorrect descriptions
Same repo listing appears in multiple files (CLAUDE.md, README.md, architecture.md, agent configs) and needs consistent updates

name: no-op-review-fix
trigger_phrases: A .claude-review-fix-*.md review plan explicitly says "No fixes required" or "PR is ready to merge as-is"
CI failures listed in the plan are confirmed pre-existing (exist on main before this PR)

name: documentation-strict-audit-remediation-workflow
trigger_phrases: Running /repo-analyze-strict on a Python library and need to act on findings
Fixing documentation issues identified during quality audits

name: dry-consolidation-workflow
trigger_phrases: Starting codebase cleanup initiative
Noticing duplicate functions or constants across files

name: dry-mojo-test-helper-extraction
trigger_phrases: Two or more fn test_* functions in a Mojo file open with identical tensor shape lists, zeros()/ones() calls, and initialization loops
A new test variant (e.g., padding or stride) would copy-paste the same setup block

name: dry-refactoring-workflow
trigger_phrases: Code duplication: Same logic appears in 2+ methods
DRY violations: Identical patterns that should be abstracted

name: dryrun-checkpoint-cleanup
trigger_phrases: analyze_dryrun3.py shows NOGO with fixable blockers (INTERMEDIATE runs, missing subtests)
Workspace directories accumulate after runs reach worktree_cleaned state

name: dryrun-process-metrics-assertions
trigger_phrases: Adding dryrun validation assertions for new fields in run_result.json
Testing that stage_finalize_run writes required blocks with correct types

name: dryrun3-analysis-fix
trigger_phrases: Analysis script reports 0 complete tests when runs are clearly finished
Orphan run counts are unexpectedly high (close to total run count)

name: dryrun3-completion
trigger_phrases: A batch dryrun left some experiments with 0/7 run_results (broken) and others with partial results
--retry-errors reports "All tests already completed" even though --max-subtests was increased

name: dryrun3-runner-failure-fixes
trigger_phrases: High judge failure count — many subtests score 0 with prose/explanation in judge output instead of JSON (e.g., "the file is empty" rather than {"score": 0, ...})
T5 ValueError: Cannot build merged baseline — T5/T6 tiers crash on inheritance when dependent tiers (T0-T4) failed and didn't write result.json/best_subtest.json

name: dtype-native-migration
trigger_phrases: You need to replace custom dtype structs (BF16, FP8, BF8, FP4) with Mojo native types
You encounter E8M0 (exponent-only) or other exotic dtype conversions

name: dynamic-rglob-replaces-hardcoded-lists
trigger_phrases: A script iterates over a manually maintained list of file paths (e.g., plugins_with_workflow_warning = [...])
New files matching the pattern are silently skipped unless someone remembers to update the list

name: dynamo-miles-grpo-self-hosted-inference
trigger_phrases: Running GRPO training where both inference and training run on your own cluster (not cloud)
Deploying NVIDIA Dynamo for multi-node distributed inference with SGLang backend

name: e2e-agent-judge-timing
trigger_phrases: Implementing tiebreaker metrics for ranking equivalent evaluation results
Need to track performance of multi-stage pipelines (agent + judge)
